title: [CVE-2025-52881] vendor: update to github.com/opencontainers/runc@v1.3.3
body:
This is necessary to pull in some security hardening added to the
AppArmor configuration, used by the "chroot" isolation mode of buildah.https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126637

Note that this depends on github.com/cyphar/filepath-securejoin@v0.5.1,
[which is not yet approved by the CNCF for use in podman or buildah](https://github.com/cncf/foundation/issues/1154).

/kind bug
Fixes: [CVE-2025-52881](https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/security/advisories/GHSA-cgrx-mc8f-2prm)
Signed-off-by: Aleksa Sarai <cyphar@cyphar.com>

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126637, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126926

comments.body:
Thanks @cyphar, that does seem to put us in a very bad spot as I don't think we can merge this due the license rules unfortunately. I guess we need to find a way to escalate this on the CNCF side.

cc @mheon @nalind @mtrmac @baude @giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat @actionmancan
should we fix the security vulnerability first and ask for forgiveness later?

Sorry, if I'd known that buildah was importing runc directly earlier I would've tried to come up with a nicer solution. :/

I'll leave a comment in the CNCF license request repo mentioning that this update is involved in a security update for buildah.

For what it's worth, if you don't need to support pre-5.6 kernels you can just use `openat2(2)` directly with `RESOLVE_NO_XDEV` and do the validation check for `/proc` being the root (inode number is `1`).

> I'll leave a comment in the CNCF license request repo mentioning that this update is involved in a security update for buildah.

I asked on the CNCF slack maintainer channel if we can "escalate" it, let's see what they say.

> For what it's worth, if you don't need to support pre-5.6 kernels you can just use openat2(2) directly with RESOLVE_NO_XDEV and do the validation check for /proc being the root (inode number is 1).

I don't think we a specific kernel support policy, given we may have to backport these fixes requiring a new kernel version may not be best.
From CNCF we are to proceed with the security fix and then ask for license exception.
Alright, let's get CI green and get this merged

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cyphar*, *kolyshkin*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/lgtm

for the bots, relying on the reviews above
